message:
Search: fix snippet truncation for multibyte UTF-8 characters

Fix incorrect truncation detection in snippet generation that compared
byte lengths instead of character counts, causing incorrect ellipsis
placement for content with multibyte UTF-8 characters (emoji, CJK,
accented characters, etc.).

Problem:
The code used `snippet.len() < content.len()` to decide whether to add
an ellipsis. In Rust, String::len() returns byte count, not character
count. For content like "Hello 世界" (11 bytes for 8 chars), truncating
to 5 characters would give a snippet with fewer bytes, causing incorrect
ellipsis addition even when no truncation occurred.

Example of the bug:
- Content: "Test 你好" (10 bytes, 7 chars)
- max_chars: 7
- snippet.chars().take(7) → "Test 你好" (all chars)
- Old check: snippet.len() (10) < content.len() (10) → false ✓
- But if content was "Test 你好!" (11 bytes, 8 chars) and max was 7:
- snippet would be 10 bytes but content 11 bytes
- Old check would add "…" even though we took exactly max_chars

The correct check is whether our snippet covered all remaining characters
from our start position to the end of the content.

Fixes in:

**src/search/query.rs**
- quick_prefix_snippet(): Cache content char count, compare using
  start_char + snippet_char_count vs content_char_count
- cached_prefix_snippet(): Same fix

**src/search/tantivy.rs**
- build_preview(): Compare char count instead of byte length for
  truncation threshold

This ensures correct ellipsis placement regardless of character encoding,
improving display for international content and emoji-heavy chat logs.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
